args.query: SELECT article_id, publication_date, region FROM article_metadata WHERE region = 'Europe' AND publication_date >= '2010-01-01' AND publication_date <= '2020-12-31';

exec_args.sql: SELECT article_id, publication_date, region FROM article_metadata WHERE region = 'Europe' AND publication_date >= '2010-01-01' AND publication_date <= '2020-12-31';

result.preview: [{"article_id": "0", "title": "Wall St. Bears Claw Back Into the Black (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again."}, {"article_id": "1", "title": "Carlyle Looks Toward Commercial Aerospace (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Private investment firm Carlyle Group,\\which has a reputation for making well-timed and occasionally\\controversial plays in the defense industry, has quietly placed\\its bets on another part of the market."}, {"article_id": "2", "title": "Oil and Economy Cloud Stocks' Outlook (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Soaring crude prices plus worries\\about the economy and the outlook for earnings are expected to\\hang over the stock market next week during the depth of the\\summer doldrums."}, {"article_id": "3", "title": "Iraq Halts Oil Exports from Main Southern Pipeline (Reuters)", "description": "Reuters - Authorities have halted oil export\\flows from the main pipeline in southern Iraq after\\intelligence showed a rebel militia could strike\\infrastructure, an oil official said on Saturday."}, {"article_id": "4", "title": "Oil prices soar to all-time record, posing new menace to US economy (AFP)", "description": "AFP - Tearaway world oil prices, toppling records and straining wallets, present a new economic menace barely three months before the US presidential elections."}]

args.code:
import json, pandas as pd

# Load metadata (Europe 2010-2020)
meta_src = var_call_mVzNTdic824pmUDXf9u5WTT6
if isinstance(meta_src, str):
    with open(meta_src, 'r') as f:
        meta = json.load(f)
else:
    meta = meta_src

arts = var_call_myRRXavrqpjOYZoCpsPhkymD

meta_df = pd.DataFrame(meta)
arts_df = pd.DataFrame(arts)

# normalize types
meta_df['article_id'] = meta_df['article_id'].astype(int)
arts_df['article_id'] = arts_df['article_id'].astype(int)
meta_df['publication_date'] = pd.to_datetime(meta_df['publication_date'], errors='coerce')
meta_df = meta_df.dropna(subset=['publication_date'])
meta_df['year'] = meta_df['publication_date'].dt.year

# Join to get text
df = meta_df.merge(arts_df, on='article_id', how='inner')

def is_business(title, desc):
    t = (title or '') + ' ' + (desc or '')
    t = t.lower()
    # keyword-based classifier
    kws = [
        'stock','stocks','shares','share','wall st','dow','nasdaq','s&p',
        'earnings','profit','revenue','quarter','q1','q2','q3','q4','ipo',
        'bond','bonds','yield','treasury','interest rate','rates','inflation','cpi',
        'economy','economic','gdp','recession','unemployment','jobs report',
        'bank','banks','banking','central bank','ecb','boe','fed',
        'oil','crude','gas','energy prices','commodity','commodities',
        'currency','euro','dollar','forex','exchange rate',
        'merger','acquisition','deal','buyout','private equity',
        'company','firms','corporate','ceo','cfo','investors','investment','market','markets',
        'trade','tariff','exports','imports','manufacturing','sales'
    ]
    # avoid some sports words if present strongly
    sports_kws = ['match','tournament','goal','league','coach','player','wins','defeat','season','championship','cup','olympic']
    score = sum(1 for kw in kws if kw in t)
    sports_score = sum(1 for kw in sports_kws if kw in t)
    return (score >= 2) and not (sports_score >= 3 and score <= 3)

biz_mask = df.apply(lambda r: is_business(r.get('title'), r.get('description')), axis=1)
biz_df = df[biz_mask].copy()

counts = biz_df.groupby('year').size().reindex(range(2010, 2021), fill_value=0)
avg_per_year = counts.mean()

out = {
    'average_business_articles_per_year_europe_2010_2020': float(avg_per_year),
    'yearly_counts': {int(k): int(v) for k,v in counts.items()},
    'total_business_articles': int(counts.sum()),
    'years': 11
}
print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(out))

var_call_myRRXavrqpjOYZoCpsPhkymD.description: Reuters - Short-sellers, Wall Street's dwindling\band of ultra-cynics, are seeing green again.
